title: Long-press to reveal contextual actions inline
design_brief: Long-press a message bubble to reveal a row of reaction emojis above it (iMessage Tapback). Reference: iMessage reactions, Slack reactions on mobile.
mobile_reasoning: Long-press triggers a reaction popover that appears above the message via portal. Each reaction emoji has its own spring scale-in.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-121, define named constants for Long-press to reveal contextual actions inline: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: For rn-121, isolate cross_platform differences for Long-press to reveal contextual actions inline: shadow/elevation, keyboard/safe-area behavior, native picker/sheet/map APIs, and permission prompts stay behind a tiny adapter rather than branching through render code.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: For rn-121, test Long-press to reveal contextual actions inline at 320pt width, large Dynamic Type, landscape/tablet split view, and notched safe areas; derive widths from useWindowDimensions or parent layout and preserve 44px touch targets.

gesture_strategy: LongPress.
interaction_details: Long-press message → popover springs in with stagger
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-121 (Long-press to reveal contextual actions inline): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Keeps continuous gesture math for Long-press to reveal contextual actions inline in gesture/Reanimated worklets, commits state once at release, and tests vertical-scroll coexistence plus reduced-motion fallback.
what_makes_this_native: 1) iMessage Tapback pattern. 2) Staggered springs. 3) Per-reaction haptic.

title: Sheet with stepper indicator at top
design_brief: A multi-step wizard inside a bottom sheet, with step indicator dots at top. Reference: payment flows, onboarding flows.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Indicator that doesn't reflect current state
mobile_reasoning: Stepper at top, content swap below. Sheet height stays constant (or animates between known heights per step).

stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-122, define named constants for Sheet with stepper indicator at top: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.

gesture_strategy: Header pan controls the sheet; close remains available as an explicit button for non-gesture users.
interaction_details: Top indicator visible at all times
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-122 (Sheet with stepper indicator at top): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Uses modal/sheet primitives for Sheet with stepper indicator at top; profile keyboard avoidance, snap changes, and backdrop updates while preserving modal accessibility boundaries.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Dot indicator pattern. 2) Persistent context.

title: FlashList with item swipe-to-delete that animates collapse
design_brief: Swipe a row, tap delete, row collapses smoothly. Reference: iOS Mail delete, Things 3 trash.
mobile_reasoning: Local state removes the id; row's exiting layout animation collapses height + slides. Optionally show 'undo' toast for 5s.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-123, define named constants for FlashList with item swipe-to-delete that animates collapse: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: For rn-123, isolate cross_platform differences for FlashList with item swipe-to-delete that animates collapse: shadow/elevation, keyboard/safe-area behavior, native picker/sheet/map APIs, and permission prompts stay behind a tiny adapter rather than branching through render code.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: For rn-123, test FlashList with item swipe-to-delete that animates collapse at 320pt width, large Dynamic Type, landscape/tablet split view, and notched safe areas; derive widths from useWindowDimensions or parent layout and preserve 44px touch targets.

gesture_strategy: SwipeRow from rn-015.
interaction_details: Tap delete → row exits with fade-left + layout collapse
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-123 (FlashList with item swipe-to-delete that animates collapse): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Uses stable keys and virtualized row boundaries for FlashList with item swipe-to-delete that animates collapse; profile scroll FPS, cell re-render count, and image/text measurement on a production-sized list.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Reanimated layout exit. 2) Sandbox 5s undo window. 3) Smooth list reflow.

title: Page transition: stack reveal with parallax
design_brief: Custom screen transition: pushing screen slides up 60% while previous screen slides up only 20% (parallax). Reference: Apple Music album-to-track, some games.
mobile_reasoning: Expo Router exposes screenOptions with custom cardStyleInterpolator (in v3 via @react-navigation/native-stack). Define interpolator using progress.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-124, define named constants for Page transition: stack reveal with parallax: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: For rn-124, isolate cross_platform differences for Page transition: stack reveal with parallax: shadow/elevation, keyboard/safe-area behavior, native picker/sheet/map APIs, and permission prompts stay behind a tiny adapter rather than branching through render code.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: For rn-124, test Page transition: stack reveal with parallax at 320pt width, large Dynamic Type, landscape/tablet split view, and notched safe areas; derive widths from useWindowDimensions or parent layout and preserve 44px touch targets.

gesture_strategy: For rn-124, gestures are optional acceleration for Page transition: stack reveal with parallax; the same action must remain reachable through a labelled Pressable or accessibilityAction, with scroll-vs-pan arbitration tested on both platforms.
interaction_details: Push: detail slides up 60% of screen
Previous screen translates up 20% creating parallax
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-124 (Page transition: stack reveal with parallax): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Uses UI-thread shared values and reduced-motion zero-duration paths for Page transition: stack reveal with parallax; profile frame time during mount/exit and assert no JS callback runs on every animation frame.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Native stack interpolators. 2) GPU-accelerated transitions.

title: Animated wave fill (water level meter)
design_brief: A water bottle or hydration tracker showing current level as an animated sine wave. Reference: water-tracking apps.
mobile_reasoning: Skia path drawn as a sine wave at level Y, animated phase. Below the wave is filled, above is transparent.

title: Rich text editor toolbar (bold, italic, list)
design_brief: A rich text editor where toolbar floats above keyboard. Buttons toggle current cursor formatting. Reference: Notion mobile, Bear, Day One.
mobile_reasoning: react-native-keyboard-controller for keyboard height. Toolbar absolutely positioned at keyboard top. Active formatting state from RichEditor's selection.

title: Tab bar that morphs into a floating action bar on scroll
design_brief: Bottom tab bar slides up and shrinks into a compact pill (just FAB) when user scrolls. Reference: emerging Material 3 patterns.
mobile_reasoning: Tab bar width and content opacity interpolate from scroll direction. When scrolling down, bar shrinks to 64px pill with just the center FAB visible.

accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-127 (Tab bar that morphs into a floating action bar on scroll): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.

title: Background blur on image (vibrancy backdrop pattern)
design_brief: A music player or photo detail where the same image is blurred behind the foreground image, creating vibrancy. Reference: Apple Music Now Playing.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: BlurView on Android with high intensity
mobile_reasoning: Same image rendered twice: foreground sharp + background with BlurView. On iOS, BlurView is native. On Android, optionally pre-blur via Skia ImageFilter.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-128, define named constants for Background blur on image (vibrancy backdrop pattern): MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.

animation_strategy: Progress changes animate through native image opacity or progress width while upload state remains resumable.
gesture_strategy: Tap to pick or capture media; long running upload work is represented as cancellable queue state instead of blocking gestures.
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-128 (Background blur on image (vibrancy backdrop pattern)): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Keeps picker/upload state outside render-heavy media previews for Background blur on image (vibrancy backdrop pattern); throttle progress events, preserve EXIF/privacy choices, and measure decode/upload latency on a low-memory device.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Native iOS blur. 2) Per-platform appropriate fallback. 3) Vibrancy is the Apple HIG pattern.

title: Map dark mode style
design_brief: Map switches to dark style at night or matches system. Reference: Apple Maps, Google Maps dark.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Always light map regardless of theme
Custom style JSON without testing readability
mobile_reasoning: react-native-maps customMapStyle prop for Google Maps. Apple Maps automatically follows system theme on iOS 13+.

gesture_strategy: Marker taps move the camera and open a native detail sheet; the same action is available from the result list.

mobile_reasoning: Skia rects per segment per bar. Heights animate in with stagger.

title: Reduce JS thread work via worklet-only animations
design_brief: Audit pattern: prefer worklet animations. Wherever JS state drives animation, move to shared values.
mobile_reasoning: Anything that animates must use shared values. JS-driven animations cause bridge thrash and frame drops.

interaction_details: Animation runs on UI thread regardless of JS load

title: GIF rendering inline
design_brief: Render animated GIFs in a chat or feed. Reference: iMessage GIFs, Reddit GIFs.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Static thumbnail in place of GIF
mobile_reasoning: expo-image supports animated GIFs natively. Tap to play/pause via prop. Cache via memory-disk.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-137, define named constants for GIF rendering inline: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: For rn-137, isolate cross_platform differences for GIF rendering inline: shadow/elevation, keyboard/safe-area behavior, native picker/sheet/map APIs, and permission prompts stay behind a tiny adapter rather than branching through render code.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: For rn-137, test GIF rendering inline at 320pt width, large Dynamic Type, landscape/tablet split view, and notched safe areas; derive widths from useWindowDimensions or parent layout and preserve 44px touch targets.

animation_strategy: For rn-137, animate only the state transition named by GIF rendering inline; keep transforms/opacity on the native/UI thread, cancel or settle on interruption, and skip motion when reduced-motion is enabled.
gesture_strategy: Tap to toggle.
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-137 (GIF rendering inline): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Keeps picker/upload state outside render-heavy media previews for GIF rendering inline; throttle progress events, preserve EXIF/privacy choices, and measure decode/upload latency on a low-memory device.
what_makes_this_native: 1) expo-image's animated support. 2) Hardware-accelerated decode.

title: Compass overlay on map
design_brief: A small compass that points North as the user rotates the map. Reference: Apple Maps compass.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Compass tied to device orientation (wrong: should track map rotation)
mobile_reasoning: react-native-maps fires onRegionChange with bearing on Android, available via camera on iOS. Use heading from camera to rotate compass.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-138, define named constants for Compass overlay on map: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.

stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: For rn-138, test Compass overlay on map at 320pt width, large Dynamic Type, landscape/tablet split view, and notched safe areas; derive widths from useWindowDimensions or parent layout and preserve 44px touch targets.

gesture_strategy: Tap compass to reset north.
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-138 (Compass overlay on map): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Bounds marker/region updates for Compass overlay on map; debounce location writes, cluster or window large marker sets, and measure map interaction FPS while selected-place details update.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Map-native heading. 2) Tactile feedback.

title: Heatmap calendar (contribution graph)

mobile_reasoning: Skia draws all cells in one canvas. Tap maps coordinates to date.

title: Modal stack from deeply nested screen
design_brief: Open a modal from a 3-screens-deep nested stack without popping. Modal is independent. Reference: any settings flow that triggers a help modal.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: router.push to a modal route (doesn't work)
mobile_reasoning: Expo Router supports modal screens at the root layout. They overlay the entire app regardless of nested stack.

gesture_strategy: Native.
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-140 (Modal stack from deeply nested screen): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Lets the native navigator own transitions for Modal stack from deeply nested screen; custom work stays in tab/screen options, with back-swipe, deep-link restore, and hidden-tab states profiled separately.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Independent modal stack via Expo Router. 2) Native presentation.

title: Drag handle on side of row to drag-reorder (without long-press)
design_brief: A draggable list where each row has an explicit drag handle (three lines icon). Drag from handle to reorder. Reference: Things 3 reorder, Notion mobile list.
mobile_reasoning: Drag handle is its own GestureDetector with Pan. Touching the handle immediately enters drag mode (no long-press wait).

stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-141, define named constants for Drag handle on side of row to drag-reorder (without long-press): MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.

accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-141 (Drag handle on side of row to drag-reorder (without long-press)): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Keeps continuous gesture math for Drag handle on side of row to drag-reorder (without long-press) in gesture/Reanimated worklets, commits state once at release, and tests vertical-scroll coexistence plus reduced-motion fallback.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Explicit affordance. 2) No long-press latency.

title: Bottom sheet that hosts a video player
design_brief: Tap a video thumbnail → bottom sheet expands with video playing. Drag down to minimize to corner picture-in-picture style.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Modal full-screen for what should be PIP
mobile_reasoning: Sheet with custom snap points (mini, half, full). Video continues playing at all snaps.

title: Recently viewed list capped at N with LRU eviction

mobile_reasoning: MMKV for storage. Insert at head, dedupe, cap at 10.

title: Inline edit-in-place text field

mobile_reasoning: Conditional render: Text vs TextInput. Auto-focus on tap. Commit on blur or enter.

title: Achievement unlock burst (badge + particles)
design_brief: Earning a badge: badge zooms in with rotation flourish + particle burst + sound. Reference: Duolingo unlocks, Strava PR.
mobile_reasoning: Reuse confetti from rn-037. Badge entrance: scale 0 → 1.2 → 1 with rotation 0 → 360. Heavy haptic + Audio play.

title: Image upload progress with retry
design_brief: Uploading a photo shows progress bar overlaid on thumbnail. On failure, shows retry button. Reference: Instagram upload, Slack file upload.
mobile_reasoning: Upload progress via expo-file-system uploadAsync with onProgress. Display progress overlay; on error, show retry inline.

title: Bridge call batching for heavy native modules
design_brief: Pattern for reducing bridge calls when interacting with native modules (e.g., MMKV, file system). Batch multiple reads in one call.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: 100 individual MMKV.get calls in a loop
mobile_reasoning: MMKV is synchronous but each call crosses bridge. For many reads, batch via Promise.all on async wrappers or use bulk APIs.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-149, define named constants for Bridge call batching for heavy native modules: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.

animation_strategy: For rn-149, animate only the state transition named by Bridge call batching for heavy native modules; keep transforms/opacity on the native/UI thread, cancel or settle on interruption, and skip motion when reduced-motion is enabled.
gesture_strategy: For rn-149, gestures are optional acceleration for Bridge call batching for heavy native modules; the same action must remain reachable through a labelled Pressable or accessibilityAction, with scroll-vs-pan arbitration tested on both platforms.
interaction_details: Batch read N keys in one helper call
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-149 (Bridge call batching for heavy native modules): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Defines a measurable hot path for Bridge call batching for heavy native modules; compare before/after on the same device fixture and fail CI when render count, memory, or JS frame time regresses.
what_makes_this_native: 1) MMKV synchronous JSI binding. 2) Bulk helpers reduce call sites.
